content:
# Acts Chapter 1 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 225-226 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_001_page_001.jpg, ACT_001_page_002.jpg

---

**1** The first account I composed, O Theophilus, concerning all things that Jesus began both to do and to teach, **2** until the day on which he was taken up, after he had given commandment through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen; **3** to whom also he presented himself alive after his suffering by many proofs, appearing to them over forty days and speaking the things concerning the kingdom of God.

**4** And being assembled together with them, he charged them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to wait for the promise of the Father, "which you heard from me; **5** for John indeed baptized with water, but you shall be baptized with the Holy Spirit not many days from now."

**6** So then, when they had come together, they asked him, saying, "Lord, are you at this time restoring the kingdom to Israel?" **7** And he said to them, "It is not for you to know times or seasons which the Father has set by his own authority. **8** But you shall receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you shall be my witnesses both in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth."

**9** And having said these things, as they were looking on, he was lifted up, and a cloud received him from their sight. **10** And while they were gazing into heaven as he went, behold, two men stood by them in white garments, **11** who also said, "Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into heaven? This Jesus who was taken up from you into heaven shall come in the same way as you beheld him going into heaven."

**12** Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day's journey away. **13** And when they had entered, they went up to the upper room where they were staying: Peter and John and James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus and Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James. **14** These all were continuing steadfastly with one accord in prayer, together with the women and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brothers.

**15** And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the brothers — the number of persons gathered together was about a hundred and twenty — and said, **16** "Brothers, it was necessary that the Scripture be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit spoke beforehand through the mouth of David concerning Judas, who became a guide to those who arrested Jesus, **17** for he was numbered among us and was allotted his share in this ministry.

**18** (Now this man acquired a field with the wages of his unrighteousness, and falling headlong he burst open in the middle, and all his bowels poured out. **19** And it became known to all who dwell in Jerusalem, so that field was called in their own language Hakeldamach, that is, Field of Blood.)

**20** "For it is written in the book of Psalms, 'Let his habitation become desolate, and let there be no one dwelling in it,' and, 'Let another take his office.'

**21** "Therefore, of the men who accompanied us during all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and went out among us, **22** beginning from the baptism of John until the day on which he was taken up from us — one of these must become a witness with us of his resurrection."

**23** And they put forward two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias. **24** And they prayed and said, "You, Lord, knower of the hearts of all, show which one of these two you have chosen **25** to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned aside to go to his own place." **26** And they cast their lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **v.1 — "The first account" (τον μεν πρωτον λογον):** Sinaiticus reads λογον ("account/word"), referring to the Gospel of Luke as the first treatise. Rendered "account" rather than "book" or "treatise" to preserve the semantic range of λογος.

2. **v.3 — "by many proofs" (εν πολλοις τεκμηριοις):** The word τεκμηριον denotes a sure sign or decisive proof, stronger than σημειον. Retained "proofs" for its precision.

3. **v.4 — "being assembled together" (συναλιζομενος):** This participle can mean either "eating salt together" (sharing a meal) or "being assembled." The formal sense "assembled together" is adopted, consistent with formal equivalence, though the dining connotation is attested in patristic tradition.

4. **v.5 — "baptized with the Holy Spirit" (βαπτισθησεσθε εν πνευματι αγιω):** The preposition εν is rendered "with" (instrumental) rather than "in," following the parallel construction with "water" (υδατι). Consistent with LOGOS rendering throughout Luke-Acts.

5. **v.8 — "to the end of the earth" (εως εσχατου της γης):** Singular εσχατου ("uttermost part") preserved as "end" rather than plural "ends," faithful to the Sinaiticus reading.

6. **v.12 — "Olivet" (ελαιωνος):** Sinaiticus reads ελαιωνος ("olive grove"), rendered as the traditional "Olivet" for clarity. This is the mount also called the Mount of Olives.

7. **v.13 — Apostle order:** Sinaiticus lists the apostles in a distinctive order: Peter, John, James, Andrew — placing John before James. This differs from some manuscript traditions and is preserved as read.

8. **v.14 — "with one accord" (ομοθυμαδον):** A characteristic Lukan term in Acts, rendered consistently as "with one accord" throughout.

9. **v.18 — "Hakeldamach" (ακελδαμαχ):** Sinaiticus spells this Aramaic term with a final chi (χ). The form is transliterated as found in the manuscript. The name means "Field of Blood" as Luke himself glosses.

10. **v.19 — "in their own language" (τη ιδια διαλεκτω):** Refers to the Aramaic dialect of Jerusalem's inhabitants. Luke provides the Aramaic name and then translates for his Greek-speaking audience.

11. **v.20 — Psalm citations:** Luke combines Psalm 69:25 (68:26 LXX) and Psalm 109:8 (108:8 LXX). The first concerns the desolation of the wicked; the second provides scriptural warrant for replacing Judas.

12. **v.24 — "knower of the hearts" (καρδιογνωστα):** A compound word unique to Acts in the NT (also 15:8), rendered literally. This divine title emphasizes God's omniscience regarding human hearts.

13. **v.25 — "to his own place" (εις τοπον τον ιδιον):** A solemn euphemism for Judas's eternal destination. The phrase is left unglossed, preserving the gravity and restraint of the original.

14. **v.26 — "they cast their lots for them" (εδωκαν κληρους αυτοις):** Literally "they gave lots for them." This is the last recorded instance of lot-casting for divine guidance in the NT, preceding the advent of the Spirit at Pentecost.

— LOGOS
